title: SpaceX 已向 SEC 递交保密 IPO 申请，承销商名单曝光
summary: 知情人士透露，SpaceX 于 4 月 1 日正式提交保密 IPO 申请，高盛与摩根士丹利担任联席主承销商，目标估值区间 $3500 亿–$4000 亿美元，S-1 最快 5 月初公开。

title: OpenAI Pushes New ChatGPT Safety Features as Lawsuits Mount
summary: OpenAI says ChatGPT can better detect signs of self-harm and violence as the company faces lawsuits and investigations over dangerous chatbot interactions.

title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
summary: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

title: Apple Mac M5 System Exploited With Anthropic's Claude Mythos AI, Researchers Claim
summary: Security startup Calif says researchers used a preview version of Anthropic's Claude Mythos AI to help build an Apple macOS kernel exploit.

title: Cerebras IPO makes billions for Benchmark but VC Eric Vishria almost didn’t take the meeting
summary: Benchmark almost never backs hardware startups. So Eric Vishria dragged his feet 10 years ago before agreeing to hear Cerebras' pitch.

title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
summary: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the iPhone maker.

title: ChatGPT Is Losing Ground to Rivals—Here Are Some Numbers
summary: ChatGPT's web traffic share is falling while its rivals are rising—just as businesses start to look beyond OpenAI.

title: OpenAI Confirms Security Breach Linked to AI Malware Campaign
summary: OpenAI says malware tied to the Shai-Hulud supply chain attack accessed internal repositories after infecting two employee devices.

title: OpenAI says hackers stole some data after latest code security issue
summary: OpenAI said the damage was limited to the employees’ devices and did not affect user data nor its production systems, and none of its intellectual property was stolen.
